  "circularTests": {

  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Race Condition / Test Reliability",
      "file": "tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/tests/admin/group/GroupTest.java",
      "line": 149,
      "title": "Test has uncontrolled race condition and may not fail reliably",
      "description": "The test `createMultiDeleteMultiReadMulti` uses a busy-wait loop (`while (!deletedAll.get())`) that reads groups concurrently with deletion. However, the reader thread may complete all reads before any deletion occurs, or the timing may vary significantly across runs. Additionally, there's no synchronization mechanism (e.g., CountDownLatch, barrier) to ensure the reading and deletion overlap, making it unreliable at detecting the NPE that was fixed. The test could pass on some machines/runs even if the underlying concurrency issue still exists.",
      "suggestion": "Use a CountDownLatch or CyclicBarrier to ensure reader and deleter threads are properly synchronized and interleaved. For example: have the reader signal when it's actively reading before deletion begins, or use a latch to coordinate deletion timing with read attempts."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "API Contract Change",
      "file": "model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/GroupAdapter.java",
      "line": 274,
      "title": "Return type change from Long to nullable Long may violate API contract",
      "description": "The `getSubGroupsCount()` method now returns `null` when `modelSupplier.get()` is null, but the method signature returns `Long` (primitive equivalent). Callers expecting a `Long` value may not be prepared for `null`. Without seeing the interface definition or callers, this could cause NPEs downstream. The fix prevents NPE at this point but may shift the problem to callers that don't null-check.",
      "suggestion": "Either return a default value (e.g., `0L` or `-1L`) instead of `null`, or verify that all callers are null-safe. If returning null is intentional, ensure the interface documentation is updated and callers are reviewed."
    }
  ]
}
